title: justify-content
description: Distribute items along the main axis.

questions.prompt: Which value pushes all items to the RIGHT (in a default row)?
questions.explanation: flex-end aligns items to the end of the main axis — right, in a default row.

options: Equal space BETWEEN items; none on the outside
All items stacked on the left
questions.explanation: space-between puts equal space between items but nothing on the edges.

questions.prompt: The difference between space-around and space-evenly is:
options: space-evenly gives equal gaps everywhere including edges; space-around gives half-size gaps at the edges
space-evenly only works in column direction
questions.explanation: space-evenly = same gap between AND on edges. space-around = half-gap on edges.

questions.prompt: Center these items horizontally on the main axis.
questions.explanation: In a row, justify-content controls horizontal alignment.

questions.prompt: The DEFAULT value of justify-content is:
questions.explanation: flex-start — items pack at the start of the main axis.
Main axis in column direction is vertical.

questions.prompt: What does justify-content: space-between do with a single item?
options: Places it at the start (nothing to space)
questions.explanation: With one item, there's nothing between → behaves like flex-start.

questions.prompt: Which pushes ONE item to the right while others stay left?
options: margin-left: auto on the target item
questions.explanation: Auto margins in flex consume leftover space — great for spacer tricks.
